mission.title: Install and Configure Flowise
mission.description: Flowise is an AI platform that allows users to build AI agents visually. It is ideal for developers looking to create and manage AI workflows without deep programming knowledge. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and familiarity with container orchestration.

steps.title: Install the Flowise CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Flowise command-line interface globally on your machine, allowing you to manage Flowise deployments.

```bash

npm install -g flowise@3.0.13

```

> Install the specific version of Flowise CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in your chosen region with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create flowise-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your cluster.

steps.title: Deploy Flowise on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys Flowise using Helm, which simplifies the management of Kubernetes applications.

```bash

helm repo add flowise https://flowiseai.github.io/Flowise

helm install flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise --create-namespace

```

> Add the Flowise Helm repository.
> Install Flowise in its own namespace.

steps.title: Verify Flowise Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Flowise pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n flowise

```

> Look for 'Running' status in the output.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Flowise deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment flowise --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 -n flowise

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Flowise
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Flowise deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall flowise --namespace flowise

gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall Flowise from the specified namespace.
> This will delete the entire cluster and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flowise
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your data and ensure your application is not running critical tasks. This process includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise

kubectl get pods -n flowise

```

> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade Flowise to the new version.
> Verify that the pods are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the image is not available or the repository is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the application logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n flowise' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Service not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the service was not created or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is created and running with 'kubectl get services -n flowise'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved UI for agent creation.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API endpoints for agent management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your API calls to use the new endpoints as specified in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Flowise UI running at http://localhost:3000 with all services operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud access configured.
